data.title: As a developer, project documentation reflects the external-dependency planning handshake and the manual:awaiting_user tag namespace
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the new planning pipeline capability. Targets: CLAUDE.md Architecture section (feature summary + config flag reference + manual:awaiting_user tag entry alongside design:awaiting_import documentation); CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (already seeded by Story A — verify and extend as needed); SKILL.md Quick Reference tables for brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan/sprint (short reference to the new behavior and flag gate); KNOWN-ISSUES.md or workflow docs if any operational nuance emerges during implementation.

**Why**: Keeps documentation consistent with shipped behavior so future agents and practitioners have accurate context without having to read through SKILL.md bodies.

**Scope**:
- IN: Updates to existing documentation files listed above; follow plugins/dso/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and style.
- OUT: New documentation files (the canonical schema contract is created by Story A, not this story); reference docs in host projects.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section summarizes the new External Dependencies feature, references the config flag planning.external_dependency_block_enabled, and documents the manual:awaiting_user tag namespace (alongside the existing design:awaiting_import entry)
- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md covers the flag and any sprint timeout / configuration added during implementation (e.g., planning.verification_command_timeout_seconds)
- When this story is complete, the Quick Reference tables in brainstorm/preplanning/implementation-plan/sprint SKILL.md files reference the new flag-gated behavior with a one-line pointer to the contract doc
- When this story is complete, documentation passes check-skill-refs and other pre-commit doc integrity gates

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow plugins/dso/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md style so updates land consistently with existing prose
- [Maintainability] Do not duplicate schema content across docs — reference plugins/dso/docs/contracts/external-dependencies-block.md from Story A as the single source of truth

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
